Supporting passages from Romans 12, 1 Samuel 24, 25, and 26, and Genesis 49 develop the application toward trusting God's providence rather than avenging personal wrongs.

title: "Sin Must Be Addressed" II Corinthians 12:20-21
description: An exposition of 2 Corinthians 12:20-21 on sin in the life of believers, drawing on Paul's expressed concern for the church at Corinth and his catalog of carnal behaviors including debates, envyings, wraths, fornication, and lasciviousness. The passage is treated as a call to identify, confess, forsake, and develop a structured plan to overcome sin, situated within a year-long series through 2 Corinthians.

title: "I'm Not Who I Used To Be" - Galatians 1:23
description: An exposition of Galatians 1:13 and 1:23 on the contrast between Paul's former life and his life after conversion, preached on the pastor's 28th spiritual birthday. Draws on Ephesians 2, Philippians 1 and 3, and 2 Corinthians 4 and 5 to trace three changes that accompany genuine salvation: a new path and destination, a new purpose, and a new perspective.

title: "I Trusted In Thee"  Psalm 31:14-16
description: An exposition of Psalm 31:14-16 on the nature of trust in God during changing and difficult seasons of life, drawn from David's declaration 'But I trusted in Thee' against the backdrop of his troubles, likely the rebellion of Absalom.

title: "In Heaven - Part Two"  Revelation 4:2-11
description: An exposition of Revelation 4:2-11 (part two of a three-part series) on the identity and activity of the twenty-four elders in the heavenly throne room, drawing on parallel passages in Revelation 3, 5, 19, and selected Old and New Testament texts to argue that the elders represent the redeemed church.

title: "Abound In This Grace Also"  II Corinthians 8:7-15
description: An exposition of 2 Corinthians 8:7-15 on the grace of giving, addressed to the church at Corinth, set within a broader series on spiritual maturity and completeness in Christ. Paul's commendation of the Corinthians, his challenge to fulfill their giving commitment, and the example of Christ's sacrifice frame the passage.

title: "Hearing And Doing"  James 1:19-27
description: An exposition of James 1:19-27 on the obligation of believers to receive and act upon Scripture, structured around the distinction between hearing and doing the Word. Draws on Matthew 7:24-29, Psalm 119, Joshua 1, and Psalm 1 to develop the contrast between those who fail to apply God's Word and those who faithfully do so.

title: "Break Up Your Fallow Ground"  Hosea 10
description: An exposition of Hosea 10:12 on spiritual preparation for revival, drawing on the agricultural image of fallow ground as a call for heart examination before an upcoming church revival meeting. Supporting passages from Hosea 4, 7, 8, and 9, along with Judges 19, frame a sustained indictment of Israel's divided heart and its parallels to the contemporary church.

title: "If I Regard Iniquity In My Heart"  Psalm 66:18-20

title: "I Want To Be Found Faithful"  II Timothy 4:8
description: An exposition of 2 Timothy 4:8 on the crown of righteousness, set against the circumstances of Paul's final imprisonment in Rome under Nero. The passage is examined through three movements: reaching, obeying, and occupying as the conditions that precede receiving that crown at Christ's appearing.

description: An exposition of Revelation 4:10-11 on the worthiness of God to receive praise, set within a multi-week study of Revelation 4 and the throne room of heaven. The passage is read alongside Hebrews 13:15, Ephesians 3:20-21, Luke 19:28-40, and Numbers 21 to examine what God receives from his people.

title: "So Moses Finished The Work" Exodus 40:1-38
description: An exposition of Exodus 40:1-38 on the completion of the tabernacle and the filling of the Lord's glory, tracing the relationship between God's specific commands, Moses' obedience, and the resulting divine presence. Parallel passages in 2 Chronicles 5 and John 2 are drawn in to reinforce the pattern of instruction followed by obedience followed by blessing.

description: An exposition of James 1:9-11 on wealth, poverty, and the call to rejoice in both financial gain and loss, drawing supporting passages from Proverbs, Luke 12, Philippians 4, and Revelation 2-3.

description: An exposition of Revelation 4:6-8 on the four living creatures in the heavenly throne room, examining their description, identity, and ceaseless proclamation. Part three of a series on Revelation 4, comparing the passage with Isaiah 6 and Ezekiel 1 and 10.

title: "Do Not Err - Part 2" James 1:16-18
description: An exposition of James 1:16-18 on the sources of blessing and the new birth, framed as the second part of a study on the warning 'do not err.' The passage is read alongside 1 Thessalonians 2:13, Deuteronomy 8, 1 Corinthians 4, Romans 10, and John 17 to address erring with respect to God's benevolence, the origin of salvation, and the expectation of gospel harvest.

description: An exposition of Daniel 9:24-27 on the prophecy of the seventy weeks, tracing the 490-year framework, the gap between the 69th and 70th weeks, and the structure of the coming seven-year tribulation as background for a continuing study through the book of Revelation.

title: FBC Oakton, VA - October 14, 2020 (Mid-Week Service)
description: An exposition of Jeremiah 10 and supporting passages in Isaiah on the doctrine of God (theology proper), presented as the second installment in a series on sound doctrine drawn from Titus 2:1. The sermon surveys atheism, pantheism, polytheism, and monotheism before establishing Scripture as the sole source for knowing who God is.

title: "Serve The Lord" I Samuel 12
description: An exposition of 1 Samuel 12, focused on Samuel's farewell charge to Israel to serve the Lord with all their heart, tracing the theme of service from the Old Testament priesthood through New Testament passages in Ephesians 2, 1 Peter 2, Titus 2-3, and Hebrews 13. The sermon develops two organizing principles: serving God as a privilege and serving God as a responsibility, grounded in the believer's identity as a royal priesthood.

title: "How Is God Working In Your Heart?"  II Corinthians 8:16-17
description: An exposition of 2 Corinthians 8:16-17 on the nature of God's work in the human heart, centered on Paul's recognition of and thanksgiving for what God placed in the heart of Titus. Supporting passages from 2 Chronicles 16, 17, and 34, Jeremiah 17, Psalm 119, and Nehemiah 2 illustrate the kinds of hearts in which God works.

description: An exposition of 2 Corinthians 12:11-19 on the apostle Paul's love for the Corinthian church, set against the backdrop of Judaizer criticism and Paul's refusal to be a financial burden. Grounded in John 13, John 15, Romans 5:5, and Galatians 5, the message traces the example, the enabler, and the evidence of love as Christ loved.
